name: Yeonguk Choo
title: Data Platform Engineer | Apache Airflow Committer | Distributed Systems
summary:
Apache Airflow Committer specializing in data platforms and distributed systems.

Experienced in designing and operating large-scale workflow orchestration systems, with a focus on system reliability, CI stability, and scalable data pipelines.

Built and operated a quantitative research platform processing multi-source financial data, leveraging automation and LLM-based pipelines to improve research efficiency.

experience.title: Committer, Apache Airflow
highlights: Delivered 100+ PRs across core UI, API, and E2E layers; reviewed 200+ community contributions
Designed and implemented Dag and bundle version change indicators in the Grid UI,   enabling real-time detection of configuration drift across distributed deployments
Eliminated flaky E2E failures by redesigning synchronization and retry strategies,   significantly improving CI reliability under high-load conditions
Contributed to Apache Airflow, a widely adopted open-source workflow orchestration platform

experience.title: Founder & Software Engineer
highlights: Architected and operated a self-hosted data platform orchestrating 300+ DAGs,   processing multi-source financial data pipelines for automated alpha signal generation
Designed end-to-end ingestion and transformation workflows across 10+ sources,   producing 20+ quantitative signals daily
Built LLM-powered agent pipelines integrating retrieval and code generation,   automating research workflows and reducing manual analysis cycles

talks.title: Airflow Contributions Using Breeze & Astronomer Agents
talks.description: Live demonstration of the Airflow OSS contribution workflow and production-grade Agent automation patterns.

talks.title: Airflow + Snowflake: Building the Perfect Harmony
talks.description: Presented end-to-end ML workflow architecture integrating Apache Airflow with Snowflake to 3,000+ attendees at Snowflake's flagship Korean conference.

highlights: Founded and scaled a 400+ member open-source community, organizing 5 meetups and 3 workshops
Built and operate a community Discourse forum with 100+ posts covering technical guides, Q&A, and OSS contribution onboarding
